quality.rationale: 宏观尺度引力波辐射估算，直接套用四极矩公式与经典动能模型，逻辑链条短但物理图像清晰，极具日常现象与基础物理常数的对比启发性

origin.question: Problem 18.1. A Massachusetts motorist shakes his fist angrily at another motorist. What fraction of his expended energy goes into gravitational radiation?## SolutionSolution 18.1. To find the gravitational radiation we use the square of the third time derivative of the quadrupole moment$$\text {Power}=\frac{\mathrm{G}}{5 \mathrm{c}^{5}}\left(\ddagger\right)^{2} \sim \frac{\mathrm{GM}^{2} \mathrm{L}^{4}}{5 \mathrm{c}^{5} \mathrm{t}^{6}}.$$The appropriate parameters for the mean Massachusetts motorist: $\mathbf{M} =$ mass of fist and forearm $\sim 2 \times 10^{3} \mathrm{~gm}$ , $\mathbf{L} =$ length of forearm $\sim 50 \mathrm{~cm}$ , $\mathbf{t} =$ period of average shake $\sim .2$ sec. The calculation in cgs units then yields a radiative power on the order of $2 \times 10^{-43}$ ergs/sec.To calculate his total energy expended, use the fact that muscles are almost totally nonconservative, so every shake uses up an energy roughly equal to the arms maximum kinetic energy:$$\text {Power}=\frac{1}{2} M \frac{L^{2}}{t^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{t} \approx 3 \times 10^{8} \text {erg/sec}.$$The fractional "efficiency" is thus $\sim 10^{-51}$ .

The calculation in cgs units then yields a radiative power on the order of $2 \times 10^{-43}$ ergs/sec.To calculate his total energy expended, use the fact that muscles are almost totally nonconservative, so every shake uses up an energy roughly equal to the arms maximum kinetic energy:$$\text {Power}=\frac{1}{2} M \frac{L^{2}}{t^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{t} \approx 3 \times 10^{8} \text {erg/sec}.$$The fractional "efficiency" is thus $\sim 10^{-51}$ .
